column1: Thinks the mysterious Main Drag spaceship just needs a snack.
Earned three NeoCreds by ‘guarding’ a vending machine nobody asked it to.
Got banned from Helix HQ for unsolicited button-mashing ‘assistance.’
Once tried to barter with Blue Silver that was actually a shiny gum wrapper.
Believes all cybercars secretly appreciate compliments.
Stares at The Core’s giant screen like it understands the lore.
Drifted into Endline camp and left with a tattoo and no context.
Found a mission brief and carried it around like sacred scripture.
Uses NeoCreds only to buy snacks, stickers, or more NeoCreds.
Sneaks into the Gaming Dome to beat its own high scores.
Once challenged a robot battle competitor to a dance-off.
Thinks laser tag is a serious diplomatic tradition.
Claims Dr. Hayes borrowed its screwdriver forever.
Believes the Midnight Market appears only for those who hum.
Calls the Promethium glow its ‘night-light mode.’
Got lost in Reboot Syndicate HQ and now thinks it works there.
Insists Kepler-609c has top-tier ‘vibe density.’
Collects mission lanyards it absolutely didn’t earn.
Uses a discarded holo-flyer as its official ID.
Once tried to pilot a cybercar using ‘belief alone.’
Won’t cross the Main Drag without dramatic theme music.
Traded a NeoCred for a noodle. No regrets.
Believes every vendor stall hides a secret quest.
Thinks robots whisper poetry at night.
Wears reflective foil as ‘anti-corporate armor.’
Says the DJs at The Core can read its emotions.
Treats table-top minis like ancient Earth relics.
Joined an Allegiance accidentally by following a glowstick.
Claims to have mapped Neotropolis but only in its imagination.
Thinks the spaceship smells like old batteries and dreams.
Licked a Promethium rock once. Won’t discuss it.
Wanders Tech Sector looking for free Wi-Fi snacks.
Calls traffic cones ‘urban stalagmites.’
Tried to debug a wall panel that wasn’t broken.
Gets too emotionally invested in retro 8-bit games.
Believes Helix scientists hide candy in lab drawers.
Claims Endline gave it honorary membership ‘in spirit.’
Hoarded 47 wristbands from previous years.
Uses a broken commlink as a fashion accessory.
Once tried flirting with a drone. The drone beeped politely.
Thinks neon signs communicate secret wisdom.
Found a single gear and calls it “Prime Gear One.”
Insists elevator music is prophecy in disguise.
Thinks The Core is run by benevolent disco gods.
Collects QR codes as if they were fossils.
Claims to have hacked a toaster. It toasted itself afterward.
Keeps a tiny pebble and calls it its backup personality.
Suspiciously adept at finding lost NeoCreds under pallets.
Once infiltrated a vendor booth looking for free stickers.
Cried during a laser-tag finale but blamed the fog machine.
Calls every security robot ‘boss.’
Tried to decode static and claims it heard compliments.
Once convinced a visitor it was staff. No one noticed.
Watches cybercars pass by and ranks them on ‘sass level.’
Approaches Promethium deposits just to feel dramatic.
Thinks the spaceship landed for snacks and directions.
Accidentally rewired a sign to blink “YAY.”
Wears mismatched LEDs on purpose for “aesthetic chaos.”
Tries to trade bottle caps despite no one accepting them.
Once spent an hour trying to open an automatic door manually.
Claims the Blue Silver glow improves its dance skills.
Keeps getting stuck under scaffolding for unknown reasons.
Thinks every festival staff member is a superhero.
Has a conspiracy theory about noodle vendors.
Calls the Gaming Dome ‘The Temple of Buttons.’
Carries a screwdriver it calls ‘Justice.’
Tripped over a cable and apologized to it.
Believes lasers taste like blueberries.
Treats mission briefings like dramatic monologues.
Once accidentally became part of a parade.
Stares into holo-menus as if reading destiny.
Doodles schematics for gadgets that could never exist.
Keeps asking robots for career advice.
Claims it once outran a cleaning drone.
Spends hours aligning glowsticks into perfect geometric art.
Believes the night market fortune tellers are always right.
Collects dust from Tech Sector “for research.”
Thinks puddles reflect alternate timelines.
Calls the streetlights ‘the Tall Warm Ones.’
Has an intense rivalry with a pigeon-shaped drone.
Insists neoprene is the fabric of champions.
Once tried to charge itself via USB.
Tells everyone it’s in ‘stealth mode’ while wearing neon.
Thinks the moon looks like a sleepy coin.
Wears goggles purely for emotional support.
Once got lost in a queue it wasn’t supposed to be in.
Thinks power cables are ‘electrical noodles.’
Claims it once tasted victory. It was spicy.
Gets startled by its own reflection in chrome.
Spends hours watching the Main Drag crowds like TV.
Calls laser-tag guns ‘friendship persuaders.’
Believes every neon sign is flirting with it.
Collects tiny screws like they're currency.
Once tried to reboot a coffee machine for motivation.
Insists the spaceship hums in a comforting key.
Brags about EVADING a stationary object.
Believes Helix engineers have secret handshakes.
Keeps licking holo-screens to ‘test resolution.’
Once declared itself Mayor of Table 7.
Thinks retro game ROMs contain ancient prophecies.
Gets emotionally invested in street buskers.
Wears a cape made of old wristbands.
Insists its shadow follows it out of respect.
Claims a traffic cone once saved its life.
Thinks Blue Silver tastes like mint (it shouldn’t).
Has a sworn nemesis: a squeaky door panel.
Believes Reboot Syndicate wrote its theme song.
Keeps trying to join robot battles despite zero qualifications.
Thinks a vending machine is secretly alive.
Stares at the Kepler night sky waiting for plot twists.
Once glued a sticker to itself and called it armor.
Believes every DJ drop is a coded message for it.
Thinks the spaceship window shapes are winking.
Has a collection of bottle caps it calls “treasure.”
Once raced a maintenance bot and absolutely lost.
Spends hours moving small rocks into better positions.
Claims the fog machines whisper advice.
Wants to fist-bump every cybercar.
Insists neon green is a personality trait.
Believes the Core stage lighting is divine judgment.
Loves holding cables like long metal snakes.
Once asked Dr. Hayes for an autograph. Got a shrug.
Keeps naming its tools after extinct animals.
Thinks the Midnight Market is haunted by good intentions.
Tries to pet every robot dog.
Believes old batteries have memories.
Once flexed so hard its LED popped off.
Calls the dusty winds “Mood Weather.”
Tries to eavesdrop on satellites.
Swears that vending-machine sandwiches taste better at 3AM.
Believes every neon color has a flavor.
Keeps trying to “hack” glowsticks.
Wants a cape but keeps tripping on prototypes.
Believes laser-tag gear improves charisma.
Once camped under a cybercar for shade.
Claims The Core music upgrades its firmware.
Asks strangers for mission tips.
Attempts to count stars but forgets midway.
Keeps getting into VIP areas by accident.
Carries a bolt it calls ‘my son.’
Tries to teach robots knock-knock jokes.
Believes puddles recharge stamina.
Once followed a drone thinking it was a tour guide.
Wonders if the spaceship is lonely.
Collects misprinted stickers like artifacts.
Thinks neon rain is romantic.
Claims its destiny is ‘mildly heroic.’
Stares dramatically at Promethium like a soap-opera star.
Once performed a laser-tag victory dance mid-game.
Keeps writing fanfiction about street vendors.
Has a lucky USB stick that stores nothing.
Thinks helmets make everyone look wise.
Once tried to trade a shoe for a NeoCred.
Claims it can sense when karaoke is about to break out.
Calls scaffolding ‘urban jungle gyms.’
Tries to greet every bouncer.
Loved by all noodle vendors for mysterious reasons.
Thinks the Main Drag spaceship is shy.
Keeps a tiny wrench as a best friend.
Once tried to gift-wrap a traffic cone.
Insists it can smell electricity.
Has recurring dreams about being a drone.
Tried to romance a vending machine.
Thinks LEDs are tiny digital fairies.
Collects lost gloves for emotional support.
Believes dance floors reveal truth.
Tries to convince robots to unionize.
Keeps forgetting it doesn’t have pockets.
Once chased a tumbleweed for 20 minutes.
Thinks the Core DJs are chronomancers.
Believes the spaceship is watching it specifically.
Once wore 12 glowsticks as armor.
Calls holo-ads ‘ghost windows.’
Tries to memorize laser-tag maps but gets distracted.
Once declared itself ‘CEO of Snacks.’
Kept waving at a robot until it waved back.
Believes street fog is dramatic timing.
Keeps trying to invent new neon colors.
Claims it saw a ghost in the vendor rows.
Treats traffic cones like royalty.
Thinks its reflection is a rival from another timeline.
Once tried to eat a hologram.
Believes the wind on Kepler-609c carries gossip.
Collects radio static recordings for comfort.
Calls the Gaming Dome its dojo.
Once asked a cybercar for life advice.
Keeps pretending it understands mission briefings.
Thinks Blue Silver rocks are edible sprinkles.
Gets stage fright near The Core lights.
Believes robots have zodiac signs.
Spends hours staring at broken circuit boards.
Once burned its tongue on a cold drink.
Tries to decode graffiti for clues.
Calls the vertical neon signs skyscraper emojis.
Thinks vendors are secret sages.
Keeps a screw just because it’s ‘handsome.’
Once declared war on a squeaky hinge.
Believes laser-tag armor makes it invincible.
Attempts to out-stare security drones.
Thinks fog machines sigh when tired.
Once tried to collect all wristbands of the rainbow.
Calls cables ‘urban vines.’
Believes tall boots improve diplomacy.
Keeps naming rocks after vendors.
Once chased a laser pointer for fun.
Thinks its goggles increase intelligence.
Collects faded posters for decor.
Claims the spaceship left cryptic footprints.
Uses a spilled drink as an omen.
Believes robots dream in binary poetry.
Keeps trying to out-dance DJs.
Calls broken LEDs ‘sleeping lights.’
Once tried to bribe a drone with snacks.
Thinks Blue Silver sings when no one listens.
Keeps declaring every alleyway ‘for science.’
Believes vending machines hold grudges.
Once hid from its own shadow.
Thinks the Core speakers are alive.
Keeps a lucky zip-tie.
Tries to pet cybercars.
Believes puddles reflect thoughts.
Calls scaffolding ‘skeleton buildings.’
Once attempted diplomacy with a laser bolt.
Thinks the spaceship is allergic to questions.
Collects neon reflections like treasures.
Believes Tech Sector smells like ambition.
Once swallowed a sticker accidentally.
Calls fog ‘ambient anxiety.’
Keeps believing it can fly ‘just a little.’
Once raced its own reflection.
Thinks Blue Silver dust sparkles out of kindness.
Uses glowsticks as divining rods.
Calls neon pink its battle color.
Believes robots appreciate compliments.
Once tried to arm-wrestle a hydraulic press.
Keeps dropping tools but apologizing to them.
Thinks satellite dishes are giant ears.
Claims the spaceship winked once.
Collects shiny trash like museum pieces.
Believes music at The Core heals emotional bugs.
Once declared itself “temporarily legendary.”
Thinks noodles predict the future.
Keeps misreading signs but committing anyway.
Believes the wind carries secret missions.
Apologized to a door it walked into, then apologized again for blocking the door.
Absolutely certain every manhole cover is a portal to a bonus level.
Invented a new emotion called 'neon sadness' and won't shut up about it.
Held a staring contest with a streetlight. Lost.
Keeps telling people elevator shafts are just indoor rockets waiting for clearance.
Treasures a crumpled receipt as proof of 'a transaction of great significance.'
Sneezed near a hologram and panicked when it glitched.
Swears there's a frequency only wuzus and vending machines can hear.
Spent an entire afternoon trying to teach a turret to purr.
Calls rust 'robot freckles.'
Wore a cable spool as a hat for three days straight. Nobody said anything.
Gets nervous around QR codes — says they're tiny prisons for information.
Insisted on tipping a free water station.
Hears jazz coming from inside walls and nobody else does.
Dropped snacks into a pothole to 'befriend' it.
Genuinely cannot tell the difference between a conveyor belt and a luggage treadmill.
Keeps a charred resistor it calls 'Grandpa.'
Waited in line behind a mannequin for forty minutes before catching on.
Teared up watching a sprinkler system and called it 'robot joy-crying.'
Describes the humidity on Kepler-609c as 'emotionally complex.'
Rode a floor polisher across the vendor hall like a hoverboard. Crashed into a sign.
Refers to all antennas as 'sky whiskers.'
Remains convinced revolving doors are time machines that only move sideways.
Named a puddle. Came back to visit it the next day. It was gone. Mourned.
Watches loading bars with the reverence of someone witnessing the patience of the universe.
Calls oil stains 'ground-level auroras.'
Attended a safety briefing and took notes exclusively in crayon.
Hums along with generators because they're 'engines that dream out loud.'
Keeps a dead battery in a pouch 'in case it wakes up.'
Left a snack near an exhaust vent it mistook for a creature.
Carries a keycard it found and introduces it as 'my tiny passport.'
Swears a fire extinguisher once looked at it 'with respect.'
Maintains a tally of how many doors it's successfully opened. Current count: dubious.
Has a whole cosmology about pipes — all of them lead to a central pipe king.
Left a thank-you note inside a fuse box.
Deeply, spiritually connected to caution tape.
Attempted to enter a restricted zone by walking backwards very slowly.
Sits near cooling fans because the sound 'is basically applause.'
Fell asleep on a warm server rack and told everyone it went camping.
Greets machines by touching them because 'static electricity is how they say hello.'
Tells time by looking at shadows. Gets it wrong every single time.
'Photographed its own reflection with a broken camera and titled it 'Thoughts.'
Navigates air ducts like they're a highway system for small ghosts.
Planted a bolt in a pot of dirt and waters it.
Sneezed once and insists the sneeze was binary.
Interprets every power outage as the planet blinking.
Drew a face on a junction box and introduced it to passersby as a coworker.
Visits circuit breakers the way some people visit therapists.
Identifies wires by smell. Incorrectly.
Stockpiles zip ties 'for the revolution.'
Asked a thermostat for its opinion on life. Stood there waiting.
Stacked pallets until someone asked what it was doing. 'Reaching space.'
Dropped a bolt in 2019 and maintains it is still falling somewhere.
Describes solder as having a personality: 'warm and clingy.'
Entered a maintenance tunnel with one accent and came out with another.
Treats spark plugs like solidified lightning.
Reveres duct tape as the product of a civilization smarter than any of them.
Tried to adopt a loose rivet by carrying it in a sock.
Sits near blinking indicator lights because it reads them as morse for 'hello.'
Licked a 9-volt battery and now insists it can taste the difference between AC and DC.
Drew a map of the entire festival from memory. It was just spirals.
Presses its face against vents to listen for sighs.
Sleeps next to a faded wristband tucked under its chassis 'for luck.'
Shouts into tunnels and waits for what it calls 'the planet talking back.'
Attempted chess against a motion sensor. Declared it a draw.
Describes hydraulic fluid as smelling like ambition and regret.
Sat perfectly still for an hour because it 'heard paint drying.'
Found a spring, wore it as a bracelet for a month, lost it, still grieving.
Imitates forklift beeps, convinced it almost understands the language.
Stares at exposed rebar and whispers 'the planet's skeleton is showing.'
Had a fifteen-minute conversation with a wall socket. One-sided.
Was accidentally promoted to 'Assistant Regional Bolt Inspector' and still brings it up.
Describes capacitors as tiny jars of trapped thunder.
Saluted a parking meter. The parking meter did not respond.
Falls asleep to the hum of fluorescent lights — calls it a lullaby.
Sorts its washer collection by 'personality.'
Firmly maintains that screwdrivers have feelings.
Ordered food from a ticket kiosk. Got a receipt. Ate the receipt.
Respects escalators — says they're 'stairs with ambition.'
Held a funeral for a blown fuse. Attendance: one.
Genealogically links all gears. Every gear is a cousin.
Had a vision inside a supply closet. Won't elaborate.
Carries a single staple in a pouch and calls it 'the artifact.'
Avoids barcode scanners — says they read souls.
Used a wrench as a karaoke microphone. Nobody stopped it.
Reads grease stains for prophecies.
Whistles at radio towers hoping for a response.
Furnished a junction box with tiny decorations. Calls it an apartment.
Mistook a thermos for a grenade and evacuated a bench.
Has a favorite number. Won't say what it is, only that it's 'the warm one.'
Wields soldering irons with the gravity of someone holding a magic wand.
Left a five-star review for a fire exit.
Treats all valves like shy faucets that need encouragement.
Dreamed in schematics once. Woke up and tried to build what it saw. Failed.
Photocopied its own hand and framed the result as 'self-portrait.'
Sits near transformers because their hum sounds content.
Brought a flashlight to a well-lit room 'for moral support.'
Feels Wi-Fi with its left side. Specifically its left side.
Received an old gasket from an unknown source and tells people it was a gift from 'a wise pipe.'
Fell asleep inside a cardboard box and later described it as a sabbatical.
Alarmed by warning labels. Not because of danger — because 'the poetry is too raw.'
Cradles batteries and says it can tell when they're sad.
Fist-bumped a loading dock. Hurt itself.
Diagnosed the spaceship's landing gear with 'trust issues.'
Wanders parking lots at dusk — says they're 'graveyards for patient cars.'
Described a snack it wants to invent but can't explain the flavor.
Flinches at fire alarms — not from noise, but because 'the building is screaming for attention.'
Describes extension cords as 'really long friendships.'
Won an argument with the wind. Allegedly.
Distrusts revolving doors. Says they judge people.
Attempted to return a sticker to the sticker it was peeled from.
Comforts all clamps by telling them they're 'hugging something important.'
Reads caution signs as love letters from buildings.
Assigned bodyguard status to every surge protector.
Introduced itself to a security camera. Shook the lens.
Gave pliers a nickname. Gave all pliers the same nickname.
Respects pallets — says they dream of being furniture.
Gauges the mood of parking cones by their lean angle.
Brought a blanket to a generator 'because it sounded cold.'
Tasted a rubber gasket and described the flavor as 'responsibility.'
Convinced every intercom voice is the same person.
Mistook a coat hook for a tiny arm waving hello. Waved back.
On a first-name basis with three ceiling tiles.
Rubs heat sinks gently and says they've 'been through a lot.'
Stacked crates into a throne. Sat on it. It collapsed.
Treats every zip tie it finds like a clue left by a predecessor.
Watches scaffolding go up and narrates it like a nature documentary about buildings being born.
Asked a valve which way it preferred to turn. Waited for an answer.
Maps pneumatic tube routes because they're 'the universe's nervous system.'
Made sustained eye contact with a satellite dish and later said they 'communicated.'
Celebrates welding sparks as 'metal confetti.'
Wore a hard hat to a dance floor. Got compliments.
Calls tool belts 'utility mini skirts' and means it as high praise.
Predicts rain by listening to gutters. Has never been right.
Studies printed circuit boards from above because they 'look like tiny cities.'
Followed a drainage pipe for two hours hoping it led to treasure. It led to mud.
Calls cable trays 'shelves for robot spaghetti.'
Was whispering distance from an air compressor and insists it tried to tell a secret.
Describes rivets as 'permanently surprised.'
Accidentally adopted a shopping bag. Still has it.
Differentiates conduit from tubing by saying conduit 'went to college.'
Accuses all torque wrenches of being overachievers.
Polished a rock for forty minutes before someone told it the rock wasn't a component.
Peeks through vent grilles to glimpse 'the building's inner life.'
Wears warning stripes unironically — calls it 'tiger-print for infrastructure.'
Left a voicemail for the moon. From a disconnected commlink.
Asked a forklift driver for a ride 'to destiny.' Was declined.
Stands outside every locked door convinced it's protecting a birthday party.
Presses its palm to the festival ground and says it can feel a heartbeat.
Avoided magnets for a full year after a horoscope told it to.
Balanced a nut on its head, stood still, and whispered 'I am The Chosen One.'
Rides inside buildings and tells people all ceiling fans are propelling the structure through space.
Empathizes with ground wires — says they 'got homesick.'
Wrote 'you matter' on a dumpster in chalk. Came back to check if anyone read it.
Tunes old radios to static and says the ghosts are 'trying to request songs.'
Ranks rubber ducks above most supervisors.
Saw the Midnight Market fold itself up at dawn and hasn't stopped talking about it.
Commandeered a gurney and rolled into the Gaming Dome like royalty.
Reads every receipt like a short story.
Aims sealant guns at the horizon and makes laser sounds.
Asked a crane operator the crane's name. Refused to accept 'it doesn't have one.'
Calls drills 'screwdrivers that got angry.'
Stands on concrete after rain, inhales deeply, and says 'the planet is sighing.'
Drew a heart on a wet window. Signed it. Dated it. Called it public art.
Mourns for wrenches that 'miss the bolts they once held.'
Gauges a noodle vendor's mood by broth temperature and is weirdly accurate.
Whispers encouragement to steel beams — says they 'have stage fright.'
Hid behind a trash can during a loud announcement and described the move as 'tactical.'
Searches for loose screws because 'every one of them is a runaway.'
Holds the festival map up to different light sources looking for 'the secret layer.'
Has an internal compass that always points toward snacks. This part might be true.
Tuned a radiator like a guitar. Got one note out of it. Celebrated.
Eavesdrops on elevators through the doors because they 'have opinions about passengers.'
Feels sorry for razor wire — it's 'just a fence having a bad day.'
Tells a detailed story about being assembled even though that's almost certainly fabricated.
Asked the sun to dim. The sun did not comply.
Presses its ear to ductwork and says the stories are incredible.
Has assigned a backstory to every broom on the festival grounds.
Solved a puzzle box that turned out to be someone's lunch container. Ate the lunch.
Cheers when klaxons sound — interprets them as 'yelling encouragement.'
Loves fiber-optic cables because 'they carry light out of pure optimism.'
Stood near vendor supply boxes long enough that someone handed it a clipboard.
Reads pressure gauges the way other people read mood rings.
Walked through the same doorway twice and told everyone it traveled back in time.
Sang a lullaby to a pile of rags it mistook for a sleeping creature.
Credits the festival's backup power to 'good vibes.'
Respects pulleys — calls them 'wheels that chose a different career path.'
Documents every washer it finds in a little notebook. Building a case.
Plugged itself into a wall outlet to 'recharge emotionally.' Nothing happened, but it felt better.
Speaks on behalf of exhaust fans — 'they're exhaling for the building.'
Heard the phrase 'load-bearing wall' and took it as a compliment about itself.
Runs its fingers along cable bumps and calls each one 'a tiny knot of stored information.'
Saluted a street sweeper. The driver saluted back. Peak life moment.
Lobbies for hinge recognition at every opportunity.
Pokes insulation foam and describes it as 'frozen thoughts.'
Witnessed two drones hovering near each other and narrated their 'argument.'
Sits near conveyor belts and says they 'secretly want to be rivers.'
Wraps itself in chain-link offcuts because the fence 'can't commit to anything.'
Submitted a crayon drawing as a vote in an event poll. It was counted.
Accuses the Helix HQ coffee machine of knowing everyone's secrets.
Sneezed and a light flickered. Will not accept coincidence as an explanation.
Flexes near I-beams and says they're doing the same thing.
Attempted to knit with copper wire. Produced nothing. Displayed it anyway.
Curates the festival lost-and-found box as 'a museum of regret.'
Waves at every crane on the skyline because 'they're clearly waving first.'
Swears a pipe wrench winked at it. Described the wink in detail.
Defers to thermostats — says they're 'secretly in charge of everything.'
Knocked on drywall and told it to 'decide what it wants to be.'
Sat on a speed bump for hours trying to 'bond with it.'
Sniffs hard hats and says they 'contain residual thoughts from previous wearers.'
Watches concrete cure and narrates it relaxing.
Hugged a support column. The column held firm. Called it 'reciprocated.'
Calls scissor lifts 'indecisive elevators.'
Posed with a caulking gun for a photo. Caption: 'Hero.'
Locked eyes with a surveillance orb and later described the moment as 'meaningful.'
Wrote a poem on a napkin and slid it under a locked door. Nobody found it. Doesn't care.
Saw a cybercar's paint catch the light and told everyone it blushed.
Wears a reflective vest everywhere because it 'grants temporary authority.'
Spent an afternoon convinced a bollard was following it. Took evasive action.
Confides in padlocks — says they keep 'secrets, not just doors.'
Walks through metal detectors slowly so they can 'sense its enthusiasm.'
High-fived a turnstile. Replayed the moment in its head for days.
Got that Endline tattoo and still can't read what it says. Won't ask.
Ties cable ties onto pipes and calls them 'friendship bracelets.'
Tried to turn in a sunset to lost-and-found.
Preaches gradual improvement. Cites ratchets as the philosophical model.
Taste-tests water in different Kepler-609c sectors and says each one is distinct.
Whispered a secret to a drainpipe. Felt immediately unburdened.
Trusts anyone in a hazmat suit on sight.
Calls pliers 'tweezers that work out.'
Found a closet and told everyone it discovered a hidden room.
Searches the Kepler-609c for 'the mascot nobody can see.'
Stares at level indicators and calls them 'the most honest instruments ever made.'
Declared a puddle an official landmark. Made a sign. The sign fell in the puddle.
Wears O-rings on its fingers and calls them tiny halos.
Jumps when a tape measure retracts — says they're 'mechanical snakes.
Refuses to work the night shift because 'it's a different planet after dark.'
Held a microphone up to a fire hydrant and asked it leading questions.
Struts across catwalks calling them 'runways for maintenance workers.'
Inhales deeply near electrical panels and calls ozone 'what ambition smells like.'
Found a note inside a panel that said 'good luck' and has been emotionally compromised since.
Calls cinder blocks 'bricks that bulked up.'
Exits exclusively through emergency exits because they 'have main-character energy.'
Started a petition to name a puddle. Got three signatures.
Holds multimeters against its chest and says they 'detect feelings if you hold them right.'
Stands still when the fog horns sound because 'the planet is singing.'
Reads every 'DO NOT TOUCH' sign as a personal dare.
Put a sticker on a sticker and labeled the result 'sticker squared.'
Ages vendor stalls by their dust patterns. Has a whole ranking system.
Flinches near motion-activated lights — says they're 'easily startled, like me.'
Walks on gravel and insists it sounds different depending on mood.
Carved feedback into a bench with a screwdriver. The feedback was positive.
Tried to turn a pulley into a yo-yo. Almost succeeded.
Got sunburned and blamed it on 'the Kepler sun being too friendly.'
Falls asleep to distant generator rumble and calls it 'the festival purring.'
Tripped on flat ground and told witnesses the surface was uneven 'on a molecular level.'
Identifies vendors by footstep rhythm. Has not once been correct.
Watches bubbles rise in drinks and says each one is 'a tiny opinion escaping.'
Lies on the road after a cybercar passes and says 'the road is still talking about it.'
Argued with an echo. The echo won.
Measures the festival perimeter each year, convinced it grows and nobody tracks it.
Lobbies tirelessly for lug nut appreciation day.
Drifts off to sleep every night to 'the hum of something thankful.'
Taped googly eyes to a relay switch and got sad when someone removed them.
Refuses to step on grates — says they're 'windows to the underworld and that's rude.'
Memorized the serial number of a generator and recites it like a mantra.
Slapped a 'HELLO MY NAME IS' sticker on a fire suppression panel. Named it Gerald.
Follows the longest cable it can find to see 'where it ends up emotionally.'
Got trapped in a revolving door for six rotations and called it 'a journey.'
Leaves tiny cairns of washers and screws at intersections like trail markers.
Heard a capacitor pop and held a moment of silence.
Wears mismatched boots and says each foot 'has its own vibe.'
Knocked on a shipping container and asked if anyone was home.
Built a tiny shrine out of dead batteries behind a vendor stall.
Stood under a dripping pipe and called it a 'shower of intention.'
Ranked every bolt it's ever found. Number one is named 'The Chancellor.'
Pressed its face to the floor and told people it was 'listening to the planet's dreams.'
Keeps a tally of every drone that has ignored it. The list is long.
Wrapped a pipe in a scarf during cold weather. The pipe did not ask for this.
Panicked when it saw its reflection in a puddle — thought it had been cloned.
Arranged forks at a food stall into a tiny fence and declared the area sovereign territory.
Sat in a wheelbarrow and asked someone to push it 'toward meaning.'
Bit into a glow stick. Regretted it immediately. Would do it again.
Brought a potted plant to a server room and explained it needed 'friends.'
Wrote a five-page review of a door hinge. The review was mostly emotional.
Stood in a spotlight and froze, convinced it was being 'chosen for something.'
Does a small dance every time it successfully opens a jar.
